title: Quiz: Ansätze for Chemistry
description: Test your understanding of VQE ansatz design for quantum chemistry

questions.question: What does UCCSD stand for?

options.text: Unitary Coupled Cluster Singles and Doubles
Universal Circuit with Single Double layers

questions.explanation: UCCSD stands for Unitary Coupled Cluster Singles and Doubles. It's the unitary version of the classical coupled cluster method, including single and double excitations.

questions.question: What is a key advantage of hardware-efficient ansätze?

options.text: They guarantee finding the exact ground state
They require fewer parameters than UCCSD
They match native hardware gate sets and connectivity

questions.explanation: Hardware-efficient ansätze are designed to use native gate sets and match the connectivity of specific quantum hardware, minimizing compilation overhead and gate errors.

questions.question: What is the main risk of deep hardware-efficient ansätze?
questions.explanation: Deep hardware-efficient ansätze can suffer from barren plateaus, where gradients become exponentially small, making optimization extremely difficult.

questions.question: A particle-conserving ansatz can only represent states with a fixed number of electrons.
questions.explanation: True. Particle-conserving (or number-preserving) ansätze only allow transformations that maintain the total particle number, restricting the state space to a specific particle number sector.

questions.question: How does ADAPT-VQE build its ansatz?

options.text: Iteratively adds operators with largest gradients
Uses classical machine learning to design circuits

questions.explanation: ADAPT-VQE builds the ansatz iteratively by selecting operators from a pool based on their gradient magnitude, adding those that most reduce the energy at each step.

questions.question: Why is UCCSD considered chemically motivated?

options.text: It uses fewer qubits than other methods
It's based on excitation operators from quantum chemistry
It always runs faster on hardware

questions.explanation: UCCSD is chemically motivated because it uses the same excitation operators (single and double excitations from occupied to virtual orbitals) as classical coupled cluster theory.

questions.question: What scales as O(N⁴) in UCCSD for N orbitals?
questions.explanation: The number of double excitations scales as O(N⁴) because there are O(N²) ways to choose two occupied orbitals and O(N²) ways to choose two virtual orbitals.

questions.question: Which are benefits of symmetry-preserving ansätze? (Select all)
questions.explanation: Symmetry-preserving ansätze reduce parameters (a), avoid states outside the physical sector (b), and improve convergence by searching a smaller space (c). They don't guarantee the exact ground state.
